description:
You are a build-/release-engineer who has just finished a local build that produced several small “mock” artifacts in
  /home/user/build_artifacts.

  The goal is to:
  1. Package the artifacts in two different archive formats (tar.gz and zip) while preserving a very specific internal
     directory structure.
  2. Extract one of those archives into an isolated verification directory.
  3. Produce a **strictly-formatted, single CSV-style log file** that summarises the sizes and SHA-256 checksums
     of the data set and of every archive you created.

  Existing source material
  ────────────────────────
  /home/user/build_artifacts/                 (directory – already present)
  ├── libs/                                   (directory)
  │   ├── libalpha.txt   – contains the single line:  «Alpha library binary mock v1.0\n»
  │   └── libbeta.txt    – contains the single line:  «Beta library binary mock v1.0\n»
  ├── configs/                                (directory)
  │   └── app.conf       – contains exactly:          «[app]\nname=DemoApp\nversion=1.0.0\n»
  └── README.md          – contains exactly two lines:
                           «# Demo Build Artifacts\nThese are mock artifacts for testing.\n»

  Required archives
  ─────────────────
  1. /home/user/artifacts_full.tar.gz
     • Must be a gzip-compressed tarball that contains the COMPLETE tree
       /home/user/build_artifacts/, preserving its relative paths
       (i.e. the archive root contains the directory build_artifacts/ with all files beneath it).

  2. /home/user/libs_only.zip
     • Must contain ONLY the libs/ directory (and the two *.txt files inside it).
     • Inside the archive, libs/ itself MUST be at the root of the zip (i.e. no leading “build_artifacts/” component).

  Extraction step
  ───────────────
  • Unpack libs_only.zip into /home/user/tests/lib_extract/
    After extraction, the following two files **must** physically exist:
      /home/user/tests/lib_extract/libs/libalpha.txt
      /home/user/tests/lib_extract/libs/libbeta.txt

  Verification log
  ────────────────
  Create a new plain-text file: /home/user/compression_report.log

  It has to contain exactly three comma-separated lines (do NOT add extra spaces):

      DATASET_SIZE_BYTES,<total_original_bytes>
      artifacts_full.tar.gz,<sha256_of_tarball>,<bytes_of_tarball>
      libs_only.zip,<sha256_of_zip>,<bytes_of_zip>

  Formatting rules:
    • The very first column is always an identifier (either DATASET_SIZE_BYTES or the file name).
    • The second column for DATASET_SIZE_BYTES is the total size in bytes of ALL **four** original files
      found under /home/user/build_artifacts/ (directories themselves do NOT count).
    • For the two archive rows:
        – Column-2 is the lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the actual archive file.
        – Column-3 is the exact integer size (in bytes) of that archive as reported by the OS.
    • End the file with a single trailing newline (i.e. the file has three newline-terminated rows).

  Other constraints / hints
  ─────────────────────────
  • You do NOT have root privileges; stay inside /home/user.
  • Create intermediary directories (e.g. /home/user/tests/) as needed.
  • Be careful with relative vs. absolute paths so that the archive structures match the specification.
  • Feel free to use standard Linux utilities such as tar, gzip, zip, unzip, sha256sum, wc, du, etc.
  • When you are done, /home/user/compression_report.log must exist and be readable with the exact layout above,
    and both archives must be present at the paths given.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files and their exact contents (already on disk when the task starts) -->
  /home/user/build_artifacts/libs/libalpha.txt : "Alpha library binary mock v1.0\n"
  /home/user/build_artifacts/libs/libbeta.txt  : "Beta library binary mock v1.0\n"
  /home/user/build_artifacts/configs/app.conf  : "[app]\nname=DemoApp\nversion=1.0.0\n"
  /home/user/build_artifacts/README.md         : "# Demo Build Artifacts\nThese are mock artifacts for testing.\n"

  <!-- The total size of the four files above, in bytes -->
  DATASET_SIZE_BYTES = 154

  <!-- Expected paths for artefacts the agent must create -->
  /home/user/artifacts_full.tar.gz         (gzip-compressed tarball)
  /home/user/libs_only.zip                 (zip archive)
  /home/user/tests/lib_extract/            (directory, created by agent)
  /home/user/tests/lib_extract/libs/libalpha.txt  (extracted file)
  /home/user/tests/lib_extract/libs/libbeta.txt   (extracted file)
  /home/user/compression_report.log        (3-line CSV summary as specified)

  <!-- The automated checker will: 
       1. Recalculate the DATASET_SIZE_BYTES from disk, expecting 154.
       2. Verify that compression_report.log has exactly three newline-terminated rows 
          and matches the formatting rules.
       3. Recompute SHA-256 checksums of the two archives and ensure they appear verbatim 
          in the correct rows and columns.
       4. Confirm that the numeric byte counts in the log equal the actual on-disk sizes 
          of the archives.
       5. Inspect the internal structure of both archives:
            – artifacts_full.tar.gz must reproduce build_artifacts/… exactly with 
              all four files & two sub-directories.
            – libs_only.zip must contain ONLY libs/ plus its two *.txt files at the root.
       6. Check that the two *.txt files exist after extraction in the target directory. 
  -->
